summary: Phase 41 — Release Gate (38/38), 9 new E2E test runners, dashboard tour endpoints, and Driver.js Role Tour fully tested. Backend: 5/5 pytest pass. Frontend: Driver.js tour confirmed visually for fresh client user — popover title 'Tur ghidat · Client', Romanian buttons 'Mai departe →' / '← Înapoi', dismiss persists flag, no reappear on reload. One real bug found: ReplayTourButton component is exported but NEVER imported/mounted anywhere in the app.

minor.issue: Response does not include a top-level 'verdict' string ('READY'/'BLOCKED') as suggested by the review request. The implementation exposes only summary.blocked (bool). Functionality is correct (summary.pass=38, summary.fail=0, summary.blocked=False) but the public contract differs from what the review claims. Consider adding `verdict: 'READY'|'BLOCKED'` to the response for consumer clarity (email body & webhook already use the READY/BLOCKED labels internally).

ui_bugs.issue: Component is exported but NEVER imported nor mounted anywhere in the codebase (grep across /app/frontend/src returns only the definition). Acceptance criteria 'ReplayTourButton (data-testid=replay-tour-btn) resetează flag-urile și retrigger-uiește tour' is NOT met from the user POV — the button is not reachable in the UI.

In the new-client empty state, only 2 of the 5 declared client tour steps have matching anchors mounted: client-escrow-info (CyclePreview) + notifications-bell. As a result the tour shows '2/3' steps (intro + 2 anchors) instead of '5/6'. The Driver `tryLaunch` filter intentionally skips missing anchors, but UX-wise a new client gets a much shorter tour than the spec intends. Consider mounting `client-new-request` and `client-marketplace` placeholders even in empty state, or move them to a CTA card always visible.

action_items: Mount <ReplayTourButton /> in at least one user-visible location (e.g. ClientDashboard 'Setări' section, profile dropdown, or footer) so the data-testid='replay-tour-btn' button is actually reachable — the feature is implemented but not wired into any page.
Optional: Add 'verdict' (READY/BLOCKED) string to /api/admin/qa/automation/release-gate response body to match the documented contract; current consumers must derive it from `summary.blocked`.

Optional UX: Ensure client-new-request and client-marketplace data-tour anchors are present in the empty/new-account dashboard state — currently the tour collapses from 5 steps to 2 visible anchors for a fresh user.
critical_code_review_comments: RoleTour.jsx (273 lines) — well-bounded; polling loop is correctly bounded (20×500ms) and cleanup uses cancelled flag in cleanup return. No issues.
ReplayTourButton calls /auth/tutorial-reset then window.location.reload() — works, but reload() is heavy; could just clear local state and trigger startedRef.current=false + force re-render.

release-gate handler requires a body (ReleaseGateIn) even though the only field has a default — consider making the body optional (Body(None)) so callers can POST without a body and still get email_admins=True default. Currently a bare POST returns 422.
qa_automation.py is over 1500 lines — split E2E runners (ESCROW/DISPUTE/FILE/CHAT/QUOTE/GDPR) into a dedicated module for maintainability.

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 pytest pass — release-gate 38/38 blocked=False, 9 new E2E codes all 'pass', /auth/me has dashboard_tour_completed, POST /auth/dashboard-tour-done sets flag + auth-required)
success_rate.frontend: 85% (Driver.js tour appears, RO i18n correct, dismiss persists, no reappear; ReplayTourButton fails — not mounted)

test_credentials: admin@propmanage.io / Admin123!  ;  client@propmanage.io / Client123!  ;  per-test new client created via /api/auth/register (timestamped emails). Sourced from /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created one fresh test client per Playwright run (tour_tester_<ts>@propmanage.io / Tester123!). No cleanup attempted — these accounts persist but are harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend all green. To re-test frontend after main agent mounts <ReplayTourButton/>: (1) login as any user with dashboard_tour_completed=true (or POST /api/auth/tutorial-seen on a brand-new account first), (2) navigate to the page where the button is mounted, (3) verify data-testid='replay-tour-btn' is visible & click → tour should re-appear within ~5s. Driver.js popovers use class .driver-popover (NOT .propmanage-driver-popover even though that class is configured via popoverClass — it's added in addition).
rca of the issue: ReplayTourButton root cause: the component is defined and exported in /app/frontend/src/pages/RoleTour.jsx:249 but `grep -rn ReplayTourButton /app/frontend/src` returns ONLY the definition line — no import statement anywhere. Therefore no JSX ever renders it. Mitigation: import { ReplayTourButton } from './RoleTour' in a layout/header/settings page and render <ReplayTourButton />.
